Fuzzers.reasoning: High-frequency anomalous payloads detected targeting input validation layers.
Fuzzers.summary: Automated injection of invalid or random data into application inputs to identifying vulnerabilities.
remediation: Implement strict input validation and sanitization on all entry points.
Deploy rate limiting and WAF rules to block repetitive malformed requests.
Patch identified fuzzing targets and handle exceptions gracefully.

Fuzzers-HTTP.reasoning: Volume of 400/500 series errors with malformed HTTP headers.
Fuzzers-HTTP.summary: HTTP-specific fuzzing attack attempting to crash web services or map API structures.
remediation: Configure WAF to drop requests with non-compliant HTTP headers.
Limit request body size and analyze logs for pattern anomalies.
Ensure error messages do not leak stack traces.

Backdoor.reasoning: Persistent unauthorized access detected via abnormal outbound connections.
Backdoor.summary: A hidden mechanism bypassing standard authentication to grant persistent remote access.
remediation: Isolate the infected host immediately from the network.
Identify and remove the backdoor binary/script and persistence mechanisms.
Reset all compromised credentials and review access logs.

DoS.reasoning: Traffic volume spike identified correlating with service degradation.
DoS.summary: Denial of Service attempt aiming to exhaust system resources (CPU, Memory, Bandwidth).
remediation: Enable upstream DDoS mitigation services.
Implement aggressive rate limiting and traffic shaping policies.
Block source IPs and filter traffic based on signature analysis.

DoS-TCP.reasoning: Excessive half-open connections detected (SYN Flood pattern).
DoS-TCP.summary: TCP state-exhaustion attack holding open connections to consume server resources.
remediation: Enable SYN Cookies on firewalls and load balancers.
Reduce TCP timeout values to release resources faster.
Implement connection rate limits per source IP.

Exploits.reasoning: Packet payload matches signature of known CVE exploit.
Exploits.summary: Attempt to leverage a known vulnerability to execute arbitrary code or escalate privileges.
remediation: Identify the vulnerable service and apply critical security patches.
Enable IPS (Intrusion Prevention System) signatures for known exploits.
Segment the vulnerable network segment.

Analysis.reasoning: Sequential probing of ports and services detected.
Analysis.summary: Port scanning or web crawling activity indicative of precursor reconnaissance.
remediation: Block scanning source IPs at the perimeter firewall.
Disable response to ICMP timestamps and other information-leaking probes.
Review public-facing services for unnecessary open ports.

Reconnaissance.reasoning: System enumeration commands and OS fingerprinting detected.
Reconnaissance.summary: Active gathering of system information to map the attack surface.
remediation: Implement VPN requirements for management interfaces.
Audit and minimize information leakage in server banners.
Monitor for slow-scan techniques and correlate with threat feeds.

Shellcode.reasoning: Payload contains machine code (NOP sleds) designed for remote execution.
Shellcode.summary: Critical indicator of remote code execution (RCE) attempts via buffer overflow or similar.
remediation: Deploy EDR to block shell execution behavior.
Utilize memory protection techniques like ASLR and DEP.
Inspect packet payloads for common shellcode hex patterns.

Worms.reasoning: Self-replicating lateral movement patterns observed on SMB/RPC ports.
Worms.summary: Malware propagating across the network automatically.
remediation: Isolate infected segments to prevent lateral spread.
Patch vulnerabilities used for propagation (e.g., SMB).
Run full-system antivirus scans on all endpoints.

Generic.reasoning: Broad cryptographic primitive mismatch or protocol violation.
Generic.summary: General anomaly indicative of custom or non-standard attack tools.
remediation: Capture full packet analysis (PCAP) for deep inspection.
Verify protocol compliance enforcement on firewalls.
Update IDS/IPS signature databases.

MITM.reasoning: ARP poisoning or invalid SSL certificate chain detected.
MITM.summary: Man-in-the-Middle attack intercepting communication between two parties.
remediation: Enforce strict HTTPS/HSTS and disable legacy protocols.
Implement Dynamic ARP Inspection (DAI) on switches.
Monitor for invalid SSL certificates.

SQL Injection.reasoning: Malicious SQL syntax found in HTTP GET/POST parameters.
SQL Injection.summary: Injection attempt targeting backend databases via web inputs.
remediation: Use parameterized queries for all database interactions.
Implement strict input validation and escaping routines.
Run DAST to identifying injection points.

XSS.reasoning: Script tags or javascript URIs detected in input fields.
XSS.summary: Cross-Site Scripting attempt injecting malicious scripts into trusted client-side code.
remediation: Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers.
Encode output data contextually.
Sanitize all user-generated content before rendering.

Brute Force.reasoning: Login failure rate from single source exceeds threshold.
Brute Force.summary: Repeated high-frequency login attempts targeting authentication endpoints.
remediation: Implement account lockout policies after N failed attempts.
Enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA).
Block IP addresses with high failure rates.

Ransomware.reasoning: Rapid file modification and high entropy I/O detected.
Ransomware.summary: Activity consistent with rapid file encryption and mass modification of extensions.
remediation: Immediately sever network connectivity to the affected host.
Activate incident response plan and restore data from backups.
Identify the entry vector to prevent reinfection.

Botnet.reasoning: Coordinated communication with known C2 IP addresses.
Botnet.summary: Device participating in DDoS or spam relay via central command.
remediation: Sinkhole DNS requests to known C2 domains.
Reimage the compromised device.
Monitor outbound traffic for heartbeat beacons.

Cryptojacking.reasoning: Prolonged CPU usage concurrent with mining pool protocol traffic.
Cryptojacking.summary: Unauthorized use of resources for cryptocurrency mining.
remediation: Block connections to known mining pool IPs.
Monitor endpoint CPU usage and kill unauthorized processes.
Deploy policies to block mining scripts.

Data Exfiltration.reasoning: Large outbound data flows over unencrypted channels.
Data Exfiltration.summary: Unauthorized transfer of sensitive data via DNS tunneling or direct transfer.
remediation: Implement DLP (Data Loss Prevention) solutions.
Inspect outbound DNS traffic for tunneling signatures.
Restrict outbound connectivity.

Analysis-PortScan.reasoning: Sequential connection attempts to range of ports.
Analysis-PortScan.summary: Classic reconnaissance behavior mapped to port scanning.
remediation: Implement firewalls that drop packets from scanning IPs.
Use IPS to detect and blocking scanning behavior.
Ensure all unused ports are closed.
